Sicarri vs Zealots

# Sicarri vs Zealots

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Feb 27, 2021

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

The Jewish [Zealots](https://www.quora.com/History-of-the-Jewish-People-What-were-some-of-the-differences-between-the-Sicarrii-and-the-Zealots-who-fought-against-the-Romans-circa-70-CE/answer/Ratnakar-Sadasyula-%E0%B0%B0%E0%B0%A4%E0%B1%8D%E0%B0%A8%E0%B0%BE%E0%B0%95%E0%B0%B0%E0%B1%8D-%E0%B0%B8%E0%B0%A6%E0%B0%B8%E0%B1%8D%E0%B0%AF%E0%B1%81%E0%B0%B2?__filter__=5&__nsrc__=1&__sncid__=12633618262&__snid3__=18016276535), were the 4th sect, in the 1st century, the other 3 Judaism sects being the Pharisees, Saduccess and Essennees. Founded by Judas of Galilee, in 66 A.D. primarily as a protest against the tax reforms of Quirinius. It also had to do with the exile of Herod Aerchalchus, and the appointment of Quirinius, a Roman senator as the Governor of Syria, and Coponius as the Governor of a newly created province of Iuadea. Judea was added to Syria, and the census by Quirinius was opposed by Judas of Galilee, who linked it with taxation and declared it as tantamount to slavery.

The Zealots came into province during the Great Jewish revolt of 66 AD, against the Roman Empire, which would also be the first of the Jewish-Roman wars, spearheaded by Judae. They took over Jerusalem, and held it till 70 AD, until the Roman Emperor Vespasian, retook it and destroyed the temple of Herod. The Zealots were noted for their violent, armed resistance to the Roman Empire, often killing many notable Roman, Greek officials.

The Sicarri were a more extreme sub sect of the Zealots, so called, from a Latin word meaning "dagger killer", because of their tactics in killing the opponents using concealed daggers.They generally operated in a stealthy manner, moving up to the opponent, stabbing him, and then melting into the crowd, pretending to be one among the lamenting masses. They attacked Romans, and even wealthy Jews who sided with the Roman empire. They were the ones involved in the mass suicide at Masada, and they often advocated combat to surrender.
